reviews.review_msg: This professor is accessible outside of class time and wants the best for all his students. He's very clear on what we need to work on and will make sure we improve almost immediately.

Nice teacher. The marking on the homework assignments was very lenient. Quizzes and tests should be okay as long as you do the textbook and homework exercises, and memorize the characters for the lessons.
I sat in on a few of Miss Jiang's classes and found she was a natural teacher. And I couldn't take my eyes off her. lol.

Absolutely wonderful teacher, I would totally take any of her class again! She is very helpful, uses awesome examples, and goes the extra mile as a teacher. It is rare to see someone who likes there job so much, and shares their passion!
shes very nice.
No Comments

Billie is clearly a leader when it comes to teaching with technology. However, when that technology has issues, even with screenshots, she will not give you the marks you earned, which can damage your grade. She is very strict about deadlines and attendance and will still dock grades even with a doctor's note. She also does not answer emails.
Multiple weekly assignments and quizzes are a lot of work but help with language retention in the long run, so it's not that bad. She doesn't quiz you on things you haven't seen in the course before.

I don't know what to say. Although the overall content of the course is easy to understand but the way how she grades are kinda making me sad. Getting one wrong on the question will bring your grade dramatically down. Everyone says its GPA booster but tbh it was pretty annoying course to even get a A-. There might be better option for GPA booster

She uses words that she doesn't teach, tells you that you don't need to know something and then tests you on it. Lots of errors throughout the course material. Grading is shady, she'll never show you anything other than the mark. Grading system is flawed, some letters have a 4% range, some have 5%. If you don't know a different dialect, don't take.

CHIN100 steadily increases in difficulty as you go along, but with enough practice I think you will be fine. Billie is a tough marker, and I felt I often had to ask for points which she should have given in the first place. I don't feel that the workload of this course was too much, but it is maladjusted to online format.

DISCLAIMER: Billie did not teach this class but her TA Ms.Tang did, however it showed on SIS that Billie was teaching so ya anyways Ms. Tang was awesome and got me so excited about Chinese I always wanted to go to class! she expects a lot from you but she's so lovely my whole class loved her, honestly the best class ive taken!

This class is easy! i don't understand why there are so many negative comments. There are quizzes which are easy if you read the textbook and as for the vocab quizzes, just memorize what she tells you to memorize and you will get a 100%

An absolutely great prof! Very passionate about teaching and very helpful too! She also shares a great deal of her life experiences with traveling around the world and it totally made me interested to travel to China to learn more Chinese!
Great teacher!

if you have a knack for languages, then this class is for you! Billie is really fun and warm hearted. She designed the class herself, I found the layout very organized and systematic. The end of the class is challenging...you may want to find chinese friends for help!
WORST COURSE I'VE EVER TAKEN
very tough
Not very interesting course.
Be careful as she can be very tough.
Excellent prof.  She is enthusiastic about the material, and explains the content very clearly.  This course was always fun to attend... definitely recommend this prof.
No Comments
She's an awesome teacher, the material isn't hard... she's soo nice and cheerful and makes the class interesting by telling neat things relevant to the course
So pleasant. Always smiling. So proud of Chinese Culture. Strict during tests.
She's really nice, lectures are super active and very interesting, definitely recommend this prof! (And it's a good gpa booster too)
I, without any hesitation, hold back, or reservation,  recommend taking any class that this professor holds.  She is a professor's Professor.  Laoshi Jia Fei rocks!  PS  Jia is a great credit to SFU!

Every day I look forward to Jia Fei's classes! She shows that she genuinely cares about her students and even does things like tell a little story about every Chinese character to help you memorize them. She has a good sense of humour and CHIN100 with her was an easy A+!!  In addition to learning a ton, I got a great grade and had fun!!
She is such a great teacher! Her class will help any beginner. There are weekly quizes which are a spelling test and grammar test which are fair game. She is always available to help you understand.

Awesome professor! Has an EXCELLENT ear, she can clearly find out who is fluent in Mandarin and kicked 1/4 of the people who thought they were so smug that they got into the class. She takes her time to help students with pronunciation. Really nice too!

Ms. Jia Fei is a sweetie - extremely helpful, approachable, and friendly. She teaches quite well. The workload is more than fair (though there is a quiz practically every class). Not a fast paced class - suitable for beginners. The textbook is simple and straight forward. A bit of practice and review of textbook will yield an enjoyable experience!

Nice teacher with awesome personality! Extremely helful when it comes to answering questions. Enjoy the small fun class atmosphere. Material tends to be somewhat repititive sometimes. A time-consuming course but an excellent GPA booster!
This class made me want to continue studying chinese!

Quizzes everyday but they aren't necessarily hard if you pay attention in class. Textbook use was encouraged but you can pass without it, albeit with a lower grade. Definitely characters > pinyin, midterm was about 50/50 characters/pinyin but final was mostly characters. Online exercises were easy.

Daily quizzes weren't hard, big on memorization. Make sure to memorize the characters and not just pinyin, midterm was largely characters. Explains everything fairly well and gives lots of opportunity to improve your grade. Overall easy class if you put in the effort (about 1h studying/memorizing per class). Don't miss class tho
